vBRIEFInfo.description: Restore diagrams and early design intent in the refreshed project docs.

plan.title: docs: restore diagrams and early intent

narratives.Description: The architecture documentation refresh accurately describes the implemented Taskfile-first and vBRIEF-centric framework, but it removed too much visual/process explanation and some of the early documents' original spirit: Deft as a modular, self-improving method that moves work from vibe-coding toward reproducible agent practice.
narratives.Origin: Operator follow-up on PR #1701 requesting preservation or addition of flowcharts/diagrams for session rituals, gates, and process flow, plus review for overly aggressive removal of early-revision intent.
narratives.Acceptance: Architecture, concepts, and command docs include current Mermaid diagrams for rule authority, session ritual/gates, vBRIEF lifecycle, source/projection flow, and triage/cache flow. Concepts restore the durable TDD, SDD, lazy-loading, and self-improvement framing without reintroducing stale path or version claims. vBRIEF narratives and generated exports reflect the restored intent. Validation passes.
narratives.NonGoals: No source behavior changes, no new commands, no MAP generator implementation, and no broad rewrite outside the documentation/vBRIEF surfaces touched by PR #1701.

items.title: Add current process diagrams for rituals, gates, lifecycle, projections, and triage
Restore early Deft intent around TDD, SDD, lazy loading, and self-improvement
Refresh vBRIEF narratives and generated exports
Validate and push follow-up PR update

references.title: Direct user request: preserve diagrams and original intent in the docs refresh
